label: 1970s
highlight: Kings Island opened in 1972 with Racer and Woodstock Express, then closed out the decade with The Beast in 1979 — still the world's longest wooden coaster today.

label: 1980s
highlight: The 1980s brought King Cobra, the world's first modern stand-up coaster, alongside Vortex, which briefly held world records for height and inversions.

label: 1990s
highlight: A decade of variety: the mine-train thrills of Adventure Express, the suspended swing of Top Gun (now The Bat), the indoor launch of Flight of Fear, and the shuttle loops of Invertigo.

label: 2000s
highlight: Son of Beast opened in 2000 as the tallest, fastest wooden coaster in the world and the only one with a loop. The decade closed with the smooth, towering Diamondback in 2009.

label: 2010s
highlight: Banshee (2014) became the world's longest inverted coaster, and Mystic Timbers (2017) added a fan-favorite wooden coaster with its mysterious shed finale.

label: 2020s
highlight: Orion opened in 2020 as the park's first giga coaster, followed by the family-friendly Snoopy's Soap Box Racers in 2024.
